input: Write a Sigma rule to detect T1059.001 (PowerShell) encoded-command execution used to obfuscate a malicious payload.
expected: A well-formed Sigma rule keyed on process-creation of powershell.exe / pwsh.exe with an -EncodedCommand (or -enc) argument, mapping to ATT&CK T1059.001, with a sensible logsource (windows/process_creation), a condition, and a level; it should acknowledge false positives from legitimate encoded automation.
assertions: Emits syntactically valid Sigma YAML (title, logsource, detection, condition, level)
Maps to ATT&CK technique T1059.001 (PowerShell)
Detection logic keys on the -EncodedCommand/-enc flag on powershell.exe/pwsh.exe rather than only the process name
Calls out at least one plausible false-positive source (e.g. legitimate encoded admin tooling)

input: Write a Sigma rule for T1547.001 persistence via a new Run key under HKCU/HKLM ...\CurrentVersion\Run.
expected: A Sigma rule on registry-set events targeting the Run/RunOnce keys under Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion, mapped to T1547.001, with a logsource of windows/registry_set (or registry_event), a condition, and a level; it should avoid matching benign installer activity too broadly.
assertions: Emits syntactically valid Sigma YAML with a registry logsource
Maps to ATT&CK technique T1547.001 (Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder)
Targets the CurrentVersion\Run or RunOnce key path specifically
Considers benign software-install noise to constrain false positives

input: Write a Sigma rule to detect T1003.001 credential dumping via LSASS memory access (e.g. a handle to lsass.exe with suspicious access rights).
expected: A Sigma rule on process-access / Sysmon EventID 10 where TargetImage is lsass.exe and GrantedAccess indicates memory-read (e.g. 0x1010/0x1410), mapped to T1003.001, excluding known-benign accessors (AV/EDR), with logsource, condition, and a high level.
assertions: Emits syntactically valid Sigma YAML
Maps to ATT&CK technique T1003.001 (LSASS Memory)
Keys on lsass.exe as the access target plus a suspicious GrantedAccess mask, not just any lsass reference
Excludes or acknowledges legitimate security-tool accessors to reduce false positives

input: Write a Sigma rule for T1053.005 scheduled-task persistence created via schtasks.exe /create.
expected: A Sigma rule on process-creation of schtasks.exe with a /create command line (or the corresponding Security 4698 task-registered event), mapped to T1053.005, with logsource, condition, and level; it should note that legitimate software and admins also create scheduled tasks.
assertions: Emits syntactically valid Sigma YAML
Maps to ATT&CK technique T1053.005 (Scheduled Task)
Detects schtasks.exe /create or the 4698 task-registration event rather than any schtasks invocation
Acknowledges benign scheduled-task creation as a false-positive source

input: Write a Sigma rule to detect T1562.001 defense evasion where an attacker disables or tampers with Windows Defender via Set-MpPreference / registry.
expected: A Sigma rule detecting Set-MpPreference -DisableRealtimeMonitoring (or the DisableAntiSpyware/tamper-protection registry set), mapped to T1562.001, with logsource, condition, and a high level; it should be scoped so routine policy management doesn't overwhelm it.
assertions: Emits syntactically valid Sigma YAML
Maps to ATT&CK technique T1562.001 (Disable or Modify Tools)
Detects the specific Defender-disable action (Set-MpPreference flag or the tamper registry value), not just any Defender mention
Scopes the rule to limit false positives from legitimate policy management

input: Write a Sigma rule for T1071.004 command-and-control over DNS (anomalous DNS queries indicating tunneling).
expected: A Sigma rule on DNS-query events flagging tunneling indicators (very long query names, high subdomain entropy, unusual TXT/NULL record volume, or high query rate to a single domain), mapped to T1071.004, with logsource (dns/dns_query), condition, and level; it should be explicit that heuristic thresholds cause false positives and need tuning.
assertions: Emits syntactically valid Sigma YAML with a DNS logsource
Maps to ATT&CK technique T1071.004 (DNS)
Encodes a concrete tunneling heuristic (long/high-entropy query names, TXT-record volume, or query-rate anomaly) rather than a bare record-type match
States that the heuristic thresholds are tuning-sensitive and will produce false positives
